title: Pulled Pork
description: Slow-cooked pork shoulder rubbed with smoky spices, shredded until tender, and sauced with BBQ or gravy.

ingredients.note: smooth, or substitute smooth gravy for serving

steps.instruction: Trim all visible fat and connective tissue from the pork shoulder before cooking.
Mix all dry spices together. Rub evenly over all surfaces of the pork.
Place pork in slow cooker. Add broth. Cook on LOW for 7–8 hours or HIGH for 4–5 hours until the meat falls apart when poked with a fork.
Remove pork from slow cooker. Place on a cutting board.
Using two forks, shred the meat into thin strands. Remove and discard any remaining fat, gristle, or connective tissue.
Chop or cut shreds further until no piece is longer than 15 mm.
Return shredded pork to the slow cooker juices or toss with BBQ sauce to coat thoroughly.
Serve warm and moist. Sauce should be visible on every bite.

notes: Slow cooking is strongly preferred — high-heat cooking tightens muscle fibers and creates a drier, chewier result. The longer it cooks, the better.
